steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Identify the center and radius of circle A
steps.content: The standard form of a circle is $$(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2$$. Circle A has equation $$(x-7)^2+(y+3)^2=1$$, so the center is $$(7,-3)$$ and the radius is $$1$$.

steps.title: Apply the translation right 4 units
steps.content: Translating right by 4 adds 4 to the x-coordinate of the center. New center: $$(7+4,-3)=(11,-3)$$. The radius stays $$1$$. Circle B's equation is $$(x-11)^2+(y+3)^2=1$$.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm
steps.content:
• "${\left(x-7\right)}^{2}+{\left(y+7\right)}^{2}=1$": Center would be (7,-7). The x did not shift and y changed incorrectly. ✗
  • "${\left(x-3\right)}^{2}+{\left(y+3\right)}^{2}=1$": Center would be (3,-3). That shifts left 4, not right. ✗
  • "${\left(x-11\right)}^{2}+{\left(y+3\right)}^{2}=1$": Center is (11,-3), exactly 4 right of (7,-3). ✓
  • "${\left(x-7\right)}^{2}+{\left(y-1\right)}^{2}=1$": Center would be (7,1). The x did not shift and y changed incorrectly. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
